prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: wide TaraHome technical blog cover for an article about safely recovering a Home Assistant Matter Server 9 upgrade that is stuck starting or shows devices offline
Primary request: create a clean editorial photograph that communicates ‘back up first, then troubleshoot the local smart-home controller carefully’ without using text or a screen
Scene/backdrop: a practical warm residential home-office or utility nook with an oak shelf, off-white plaster wall, linen texture, one restrained dark-green notebook or ceramic accent, and a softly glowing household lamp in the background
Subject: a compact unbranded local smart-home controller on the oak shelf beside a small external backup drive, one tidy Ethernet cable, and a homeowner’s hand paused just above the controller rather than pressing or unplugging it; believable everyday electronics, not a recognizable commercial product
Style/medium: photorealistic editorial interior photography, natural material grain and small real-life imperfections, not CGI
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape; slight three-quarter eye-level view; keep the controller, backup drive, and paused hand clearly recognizable inside the central 70 percent so the article-card crop remains meaningful; shallow but not extreme depth of field
Lighting/mood: soft late-afternoon window light mixed with the warm lamp, calm, practical, careful, true-to-life color
Color palette: warm oak and cream with restrained dark-green accents and small neutral black electronics details
Constraints: no brand logo; no words or numbers; no visible screen; no app interface; no embedded headline or title poster; no fake chart or graph; no decorative routing, network, or path lines; no translucent panel; no inset frame; no floating UI card; no progress bar; no watermark; no repeated beige device diagram; physically plausible ports and cable placement
Avoid: glossy CGI, generic AI diagram look, over-staged luxury showroom, science-fiction hardware, alarm imagery, excessive cables, floating objects, invented branded hardware, product-ad composition
